description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the default executor with a global before_script that runs apt-get update -qy, installs python3 python3-pip sudo postgresql postgresql-client, stops and starts the postgresql service, executes sudo -u postgres psql -c "ALTER USER postgres WITH PASSWORD 'password'", creates the lipsy database with sudo -u postgres psql -c "CREATE DATABASE lipsy OWNER postgres", installs Python packages via pip3 install -r req.txt, and runs python3 lipsy/manage.py makemigrations and python3 lipsy/manage.py migrate. Define two stages, test and deploy, with a test job that cd's into lipsy, runs coverage run --rcfile=.coveragerc manage.py test, then coverage report, and returns to the project root. Add a deploy job that depends on test, runs only on the develop branch, installs sshpass, then uses sshpass -p "$Autodeploy" to scp the lipsy directory to root@139.59.231.183:~/lipsy, starts the Docker container named lipsy, removes old migration files, restarts services, drops and recreates the remote lipsy database, runs remote Django makemigrations, migrate, loads fixture with loaddata, clears and collects static files, and finally restarts Apache on the remote host.
